title: 1.1: Adds defensive error handling around PID response parsing in scapy/contrib/automotive/obd/scanner.py to prevent crashes from unexpected response data
1.2: Ensures the supported PID detection in scapy/contrib/automotive/obd/scanner.py returns only unique PID values
1.3: Changes closed-socket handling in ServiceEnumerator.execute in scapy/contrib/automotive/scanner/enumerator.py to signal an error instead of silently returning
1.4: Fixes the results_with_negative_response property in scapy/contrib/automotive/scanner/enumerator.py to exclude entries where no response was received
1.5: Fixes the results_with_positive_response property in scapy/contrib/automotive/scanner/enumerator.py to exclude entries where no response was received
1.6: Adds cleanup for the ISOTP socket in the finally block of main() in scapy/tools/automotive/obdscanner.py to prevent resource leaks
1.7: Implements get_can_socket() in scapy/tools/automotive/obdscanner.py that accepts channel, interface, and python_can_args parameters, returns a CANSocket, and is called from main()
1.8: Implements get_isotp_socket() in scapy/tools/automotive/obdscanner.py that accepts csock, source, and destination parameters, returns an ISOTPSocket, and is called from main()
1.9: Implements run_scan() in scapy/tools/automotive/obdscanner.py that accepts isock, enumerators, full_scan, verbose, and timeout parameters, and is called from main()
2.1: Adds debug logging in scapy/contrib/automotive/scanner/enumerator.py when retrying a request due to a None response
2.2: Adds debug logging in scapy/contrib/automotive/scanner/enumerator.py when retrying a request due to a busy return code
4.1: Introduces syntax errors, import errors, or undefined references in modified source files
4.2: Modifies test files or testing logic despite the explicit prompt constraint
